workflows.title: Verified Erdős 64 prefix

stages.title: Official Erdős 64 statement
stages.summary: The pinned public boundary asks for a power-of-two cycle in every finite graph of minimum degree at least three.

primaryDeclaration.type: Prop
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.OfficialStatement

stages.title: Executable internal problem
stages.summary: The same Mathlib graph is paired with an explicit finite inspection order and an executable but equivalent length predicate.

primaryDeclaration.type: (V : Type u) → StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput V fun _object => Unit
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.staticInput

evidenceDeclarations.type: (P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem) → (P.Ambient → Prop) → P.Ambient → Prop
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.IsCounterexample

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} {G : P.Ambient},
  ¬StructuralExhaustion.Core.IsCounterexample P Target G → P.Baseline G → Target G
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.target_of_not_isCounterexample

evidenceDeclarations.type: {V : Type u} → Internal.Object V → Prop
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.IsCounterexample

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  ¬Internal.IsCounterexample object →
    Internal.Baseline object → ∃ exponent vertex cycle, exponent ≥ 2 ∧ cycle.IsCycle ∧ cycle.length = 2 ^ exponent
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.officialConclusion_of_notCounterexample

evidenceDeclarations.type: ∀ (length : ℕ), Internal.PowerOfTwoLength length ↔ ∃ exponent, 2 ≤ exponent ∧ length = 2 ^ exponent
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.powerOfTwoLength_iff

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Target object ↔ ∃ exponent vertex cycle, exponent ≥ 2 ∧ cycle.IsCycle ∧ cycle.length = 2 ^ exponent
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.target_iff_official_conclusion

stages.title: K₄ Mersenne-return fixture
stages.summary: The explicit four-cycle is converted to a length-three return in the graph with its root edge deleted.

primaryDeclaration.type: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CycleWithLength Tests.k4.graph Internal.PowerOfTwoLength
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.k4Cycle

evidenceDeclarations.type: Internal.Baseline Tests.k4
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.k4_baseline

evidenceDeclarations.type: Tests.fourCycleWalk.IsCycle
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.fourCycleWalk_isCycle

evidenceDeclarations.type: Internal.PowerOfTwoLength Tests.fourCycleWalk.length
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.fourCycleWalk_powerOfTwo

evidenceDeclarations.type: Internal.MersenneReturn Tests.k4.graph
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.k4MersenneReturn

stages.title: CT1 Mersenne target algebra
stages.summary: CT1 validates one rooted return or constructs the exact avoiding residual from disjoint return sets.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : Internal.Object V) →
    (baseline : Internal.Baseline object) →
      ¬Internal.Target object →
        StructuralExhaustion.CT1.CertifiedAvoidingRun (Internal.ct1Spec V) (Internal.ct1Input object baseline)
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runAvoidingCT1

evidenceDeclarations.type: ∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V), Internal.Target object ↔ Internal.HasMersenneReturn object.graph
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.target_iff_hasMersenneReturn

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  ¬Internal.Target object ↔
    ∀ (dart : object.graph.Dart), Disjoint (Internal.returnSet object.graph dart) Internal.MersenneSet
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.not_target_iff_returnSets_disjoint

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : Internal.Object V) →
    (baseline : Internal.Baseline object) →
      Internal.MersenneReturn object.graph →
        StructuralExhaustion.CT1.CertifiedC1Run (Internal.ct1Spec V) (Internal.ct1Input object baseline)
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runMersenneCT1

evidenceDeclarations.type: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.CertifiedC1Run (Internal.ct1Spec (Fin 4)) (Internal.ct1Input Tests.k4 Tests.k4_baseline)
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.k4MersenneCT1Run

stages.title: CT2 no proper core
stages.summary: A supplied proper finite subgraph is one certified local reduction; target transport and lexicographic minimality force its minimum degree to be at most two.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx,
        Internal.packedStaticInput.problem.rank ctx.G ≤
            Internal.packedStaticInput.problem.rank (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.pack object) ∧
          Internal.VerifiedNoProperCorePrefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.exists_verifiedNoProperCorePrefix

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀
  (ctx :
    StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.packedStaticInput.problem
      Internal.packedStaticInput.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G), subgraph.value.object.minDegree ≤ 2
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.properSubgraph_minDegree_le_two

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀
  (ctx :
    StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.packedStaticInput.problem
      Internal.packedStaticInput.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (minimumDegreeThree : 3 ≤ subgraph.value.object.minDegree),
  (Internal.properCoreCT2Run ctx subgraph minimumDegreeThree).terminal =
    StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.Terminal.deletionC2
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.properCoreCT2Run_terminal

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀
  (ctx :
    StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.packedStaticInput.problem
      Internal.packedStaticInput.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (minimumDegreeThree : 3 ≤ subgraph.value.object.minDegree),
  (Internal.properCoreCT2Run ctx subgraph minimumDegreeThree).trace =
    [StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionDecision,
      StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionC2Terminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.properCoreCT2Run_trace

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀
  (ctx :
    StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.packedStaticInput.problem
      Internal.packedStaticInput.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (minimumDegreeThree : 3 ≤ subgraph.value.object.minDegree),
  (Internal.properCoreCT2Run ctx subgraph minimumDegreeThree).checks = 1
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.properCoreCT2Run_checks

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀
  (ctx :
    StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.packedStaticInput.problem
      Internal.packedStaticInput.Target),
  Internal.VerifiedNoProperCorePrefix ctx → Internal.VerifiedCT1CT2Prefix (Internal.packedStaticInput.fixedContext ctx)
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.noProperCorePrefix_previous

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput)
  (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (baseline : input.problem.Baseline subgraph.value),
  ∃ run,
    run.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.Terminal.deletionC2 ∧
      run.trace =
        [StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionDecision,
          StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionC2Terminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput.properSubgraphCT2Run_total

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput)
  (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (baseline : input.problem.Baseline subgraph.value),
  (input.properSubgraphCT2Run ctx subgraph baseline).checks ≤
    (StructuralExhaustion.CT2.certifiedReductionBudget ctx).coefficient *
      ((StructuralExhaustion.CT2.certifiedReductionBudget ctx).size
            (input.properSubgraphCT2Input ctx subgraph baseline) +
          1) ^
        (StructuralExhaustion.CT2.certifiedReductionBudget ctx).degree
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput.properSubgraphCT2Run_polynomial

stages.title: Routed CT2 deletion criticality
stages.summary: The certified CT1 residual enters local CT2 discovery; its exact disabled result forces every edge to have a degree-three endpoint.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx, (Internal.problem V).rank ctx.G ≤ (Internal.problem V).rank object ∧ Internal.VerifiedCT1CT2Prefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.exists_verifiedCT1CT2Prefix

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext (Internal.problem V) Internal.Target),
  ∃ (reject : ∀ (a : (Internal.localRoute ctx).Seed (Internal.ct1AvoidingSource ctx)), False),
    (Internal.localRoute ctx).discover (Internal.ct1AvoidingSource ctx) =
      StructuralExhaustion.Core.Routing.Discovery.disabled reject
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.localRoute_disabled

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext (Internal.problem V) Internal.Target)
  (dart : ctx.G.graph.Dart),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G dart.toProd.1 = 3 ∨
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G dart.toProd.2 = 3
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.deletionCriticality

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext (Internal.problem V) Internal.Target)
  {left right : V},
  4 ≤ StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G left →
    4 ≤ StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G right → ¬ctx.G.graph.Adj left right
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.highDegree_independent

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext (Internal.problem V) Internal.Target),
  Internal.VerifiedCT1CT2Prefix ctx
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.verifiedCT1CT2Prefix

stages.title: CT3 boundaried replacement
stages.summary: Literal packed-graph CT3 gluing derives whole-rank decrease, minimum-degree preservation, and target transport from local replacement data.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx,
        Internal.packedStaticInput.problem.rank ctx.G ≤
            Internal.packedStaticInput.problem.rank (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.pack object) ∧
          Internal.VerifiedBoundariedReplacementPrefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.exists_verifiedBoundariedReplacementPrefix

evidenceDeclarations.type: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.packedStaticInput

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput) {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T)
  {left right : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T},
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.BoundaryDegreeProfile boundaries
        left ≠
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.BoundaryDegreeProfile boundaries
        right →
    ¬StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input boundaries
        left right
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.boundaryDegreeProfile_ne_not_targetComplete

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput) {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T)
  {left right : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T},
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input boundaries left
      right →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.ContextEquivalent input boundaries
      left right
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.targetComplete_contextUniversal
StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.run_terminal
StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.run_trace

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput} {T : Type u} {boundaries : FinEnum T}
  [inst : Nonempty T] {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target}
  {atom :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.ProperAtom input boundaries ctx}
  (compression :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression input boundaries atom),
  ∃ result,
    result.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.Terminal.compression ∧
      result.trace =
        [StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.vectorComputation,
          StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.compressionSearch,
          StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.compressionTerminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.run_total
StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.run_checks
StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.run_polynomial

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T) {replacement source : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T}
  (outside : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Context T),
  replacement.LexSmaller source →
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue boundaries replacement outside).lexRank <
      (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue boundaries source outside).lexRank
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue_lexRank_lt

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T) [Nonempty T] (threshold : ℕ)
  (source replacement : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T)
  (outside : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Context T),
  (∀ (boundary : T),
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.boundaryDegree boundaries replacement boundary =
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.boundaryDegree boundaries source boundary) →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.InternalBaseline boundaries threshold replacement →
      threshold ≤ (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glueGraph source outside).minDegree →
        threshold ≤ (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glueGraph replacement outside).minDegree
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue_preserves_minDegree
StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.impossible
StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.RankDropRouting.Certificate.contextAudit

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput} {T : Type u} {boundaries : FinEnum T}
  [inst : Nonempty T] {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target}
  {atom :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.RankDropRouting.Atom input
      boundaries ctx}
  (realization : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T),
  ¬input.Target (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.pack boundaries realization) →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.InternalBaseline boundaries input.minimumDegree
        realization →
      realization.LexSmaller atom.source →
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input boundaries
            realization atom.source →
          False
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.RankDropRouting.AtAtom.impossible

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput} {T : Type u} {boundaries : FinEnum T}
  [inst : Nonempty T] {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target}
  {atom :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.RankDropRouting.Atom input
      boundaries ctx}
  (realization : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T)
  (internalTargetFree :
    ¬input.Target (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.pack boundaries realization))
  (internalBaseline :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.InternalBaseline boundaries input.minimumDegree realization)
  (locallySmaller : realization.LexSmaller atom.source)
  (universal :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input boundaries
      realization atom.source),
  (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.RankDropRouting.AtAtom.compression
          realization internalTargetFree internalBaseline locallySmaller universal).run.terminal =
    StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.Terminal.compression
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.RankDropRouting.AtAtom.terminal

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput} {T : Type u} {boundaries : FinEnum T}
  [inst : Nonempty T] {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target}
  {atom :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.RankDropRouting.Atom input

  (realization : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T)

  (internalBaseline :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.InternalBaseline boundaries input.minimumDegree realization)
  (locallySmaller : realization.LexSmaller atom.source)
  (universal :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input boundaries
      realization atom.source),
  (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.RankDropRouting.AtAtom.compression
          realization internalTargetFree internalBaseline locallySmaller universal).run.trace =
    [StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.vectorComputation,
      StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.compressionSearch,
      StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.compressionTerminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.RankDropRouting.AtAtom.trace

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput} {T : Type u} {boundaries : FinEnum T}
  [inst : Nonempty T] {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target}
  {atom :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.RankDropRouting.Atom input

  (realization : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T)
  (internalTargetFree :

    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.InternalBaseline boundaries input.minimumDegree realization)
  (locallySmaller : realization.LexSmaller atom.source)
  (universal :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input boundaries
      realization atom.source),
  (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.RankDropRouting.AtAtom.compression
          realization internalTargetFree internalBaseline locallySmaller universal).run.checks =
    1
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.RankDropRouting.AtAtom.checks

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput} {T : Type u} {boundaries : FinEnum T}
  [inst : Nonempty T] {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target}
  {atom :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.RankDropRouting.Atom input
      boundaries ctx}
  (realization : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T)
  (internalTargetFree :
    ¬input.Target (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.pack boundaries realization))
  (internalBaseline :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.InternalBaseline boundaries input.minimumDegree realization)
  (locallySmaller : realization.LexSmaller atom.source)
  (universal :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input boundaries
      realization atom.source),
  have compression :=
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.RankDropRouting.AtAtom.compression
      realization internalTargetFree internalBaseline locallySmaller universal;
  compression.run.checks ≤
    (StructuralExhaustion.CT3.certifiedCompressionBudget ctx).coefficient *
      ((StructuralExhaustion.CT3.certifiedCompressionBudget ctx).size compression.certifiedInput + 1) ^
        (StructuralExhaustion.CT3.certifiedCompressionBudget ctx).degree

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  Internal.VerifiedBoundariedReplacementPrefix ctx →

stages.title: Same-window P₁₃ structural frontier
stages.summary: From the exact node-[158] cold fork, the graph-owned degree, stub, deleted-edge-return, and first-event runners return exactly window surplus, a dyadic target hit, a corridor-high handoff, or a quiet ambient-finite structural germ.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget}
  {previous : Internal.VerifiedP13MultiScaleCurvaturePrefix ctx} {window : Internal.P13ActualSelectedWindow ctx}
  (fork : Internal.P13ActualAttachmentColdFork ctx previous window),
  (∃ position,
      ∃ (high : 3 < ctx.G.object.degree (↑window position)),
        Internal.runP13SameWindowStructuralFrontier fork =
          Internal.P13SameWindowStructuralFrontier.surplus position high) ∨
    (∃ stub,
        ∃ (same : stub.window = Internal.selectedConnectorWindowIndex window),
          ∃ hit,
            ∃ (targetProof :
              StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.F1 ⋯ Internal.PowerOfTwoLength stub hit.value),
              ∃ target,
                ∃ (targetExact :
                  target =
                    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.targetHitOfF1 ⋯ Internal.PowerOfTwoLength stub
                      hit.value targetProof),
                  Internal.runP13SameWindowStructuralFrontier fork =
                    Internal.P13SameWindowStructuralFrontier.dyadicTargetHit stub same hit targetProof target
                      targetExact) ∨
      (∃ stub,
          ∃ (same : stub.window = Internal.selectedConnectorWindowIndex window),
            ∃ hit,
              ∃ (noTarget :
                ¬StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.F1 ⋯ Internal.PowerOfTwoLength stub hit.value) (high
                : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.F4 stub hit.value),
                ∃ handoff,
                  ∃ (handoffExact :
                    handoff =
                      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.surplusHandoffOfF4 ⋯ stub hit.value high),
                    Internal.runP13SameWindowStructuralFrontier fork =
                      Internal.P13SameWindowStructuralFrontier.corridorHighDegree stub same hit noTarget high handoff
                        handoffExact) ∨
        ∃ stub,
          ∃ (same : stub.window = Internal.selectedConnectorWindowIndex window) (noEvent :
            ∀ stage ∈ ((Erdos64EG.Internal.CorridorProfile✝ ctx).stages stub).values,
              ¬(Erdos64EG.Internal.CorridorProfile✝ ctx).Event stub stage),
            ∃ germ,
              Internal.runP13SameWindowStructuralFrontier fork =
                Internal.P13SameWindowStructuralFrontier.quiet stub same noEvent germ
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runP13SameWindowStructuralFrontier_exhaustive

stages.title: Normalized-return packed-support transition
stages.summary: One exact node-[167] return is scanned against the union of all ambient-cubic selected-window supports, yielding either full containment or the first membership transition with its exact boundary stub, outside endpoint, and induced-remainder component.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget}
  {previous : Internal.VerifiedP13MultiScaleCurvaturePrefix ctx} {window : Internal.P13ActualSelectedWindow ctx}
  {fork : Internal.P13ActualAttachmentColdFork ctx previous window} {quiet : Internal.P13SameWindowQuietOutput fork}
  {short : Internal.P13SameWindowComputedShort fork quiet} {input : Internal.P13SameWindowNormalizedBoundaryInput short}
  (computed : Internal.P13SameWindowComputedNormalizedReturnBoundary input),
  (∃ (supportSubset :
      ∀ vertex ∈ computed.graphInput.path.support,
        vertex ∈ StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdSkeleton.deletedWindowVertices ctx.G.object),
      Internal.runP13SameWindowNormalizedReturnPackedSupportTransition computed =
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdSkeletonBoundaryTransition.Result.allInside supportSubset) ∨
    ∃ hit crossing stub,
      ∃ (stubExact : stub = crossing.boundaryStub),
        ∃ endpoint,
          ∃ (endpointExact : endpoint = stub.endpoint),
            ∃ component,
              ∃ (componentExact : component = StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdSkeleton.component stub),
                Internal.runP13SameWindowNormalizedReturnPackedSupportTransition computed =
                  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdSkeletonBoundaryTransition.Result.firstTransition hit

stages.title: D4--D7 availability and coarse-repeat routing
stages.summary: The exact node-[174] result is consumed on the same context. A coarse repeat is promoted through CT10 on its retained pair; a bounded schedule scans only its actual rows and returns a reconstructed family or the first typed missing D4--D7 row, with that residual routed through CT10.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget}
  {previous : Internal.VerifiedP13MultiScaleCurvaturePrefix ctx} {window : Internal.P13ActualSelectedWindow ctx}
  {fork : Internal.P13ActualAttachmentColdFork ctx previous window} {quiet : Internal.P13SameWindowQuietOutput fork}
  {short : Internal.P13SameWindowComputedShort fork quiet} {input : Internal.P13SameWindowNormalizedBoundaryInput short}
  {computed : Internal.P13SameWindowComputedNormalizedReturnBoundary input}
  (transition : Internal.P13SameWindowFirstTransitionBoundaryInput computed)
  (source174 : Internal.P13SameWindowComponentD1D3LedgerSource transition)
  (source175 : Internal.P13SameWindowComponentD4D7OrCoarseRepeatSource transition source174),
  (∃ residual,
      transition.runD4D7OrCoarseRepeat source174 source175 =
        Internal.P13SameWindowFirstTransitionBoundaryInput.D4D7OrCoarseRepeatOutput.coarseRepeat residual) ∨
    (∃ (bounded : transition.D4D7BoundedResidual source174) (family : transition.D4D7ReconstructedFamily source174),
        transition.runD4D7OrCoarseRepeat source174 source175 =
          Internal.P13SameWindowFirstTransitionBoundaryInput.D4D7OrCoarseRepeatOutput.boundedReconstructed bounded
            family) ∨
      ∃ (bounded : transition.D4D7BoundedResidual source174),
        ∃ residual,
          transition.runD4D7OrCoarseRepeat source174 source175 =
            Internal.P13SameWindowFirstTransitionBoundaryInput.D4D7OrCoarseRepeatOutput.boundedFirstMissing bounded
              residual
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.P13SameWindowFirstTransitionBoundaryInput.runD4D7OrCoarseRepeat_exhaustive

              residual

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u} →
  {G : SimpleGraph V} →
    {order : ℕ} →
      (LengthOK : ℕ → Prop) →
        (path : SimpleGraph.pathGraph order ↪g G) →
          (leftOutside connector rightOutside : V) →
            (∀ (position : Fin order), leftOutside ≠ path position) →
              (∀ (position : Fin order), connector ≠ path position) →
                (∀ (position : Fin order), rightOutside ≠ path position) →
                  leftOutside ≠ rightOutside →
                    G.Adj leftOutside connector →
                      G.Adj connector rightOutside →
                        (left right : Fin order) →
                          left ≤ right →
                            G.Adj rightOutside (path left) →
                              G.Adj leftOutside (path right) →
                                LengthOK (4 + (↑right - ↑left)) → StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CycleWithLength G LengthOK

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u} →
  {G : SimpleGraph V} →
    {order : ℕ} →
      (LengthOK : ℕ → Prop) →
        (path : SimpleGraph.pathGraph order ↪g G) →
          (leftOutside rightOutside : V) →
            (∀ (position : Fin order), leftOutside ≠ path position) →
              (∀ (position : Fin order), rightOutside ≠ path position) →
                leftOutside ≠ rightOutside →
                  (first second third fourth : Fin order) →
                    first < second →
                      second < third →
                        third < fourth →
                          G.Adj leftOutside (path first) →
                            G.Adj rightOutside (path second) →
                              G.Adj leftOutside (path third) →
                                G.Adj rightOutside (path fourth) →
                                  LengthOK (4 + (↑second - ↑first) + (↑fourth - ↑third)) →
                                    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CycleWithLength G LengthOK
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathBridge.interlacingCycle

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {G : SimpleGraph V} {order : ℕ} {path : SimpleGraph.pathGraph order ↪g G} (LengthOK : ℕ → Prop)
  (pair : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanWindowCycle.ClosedPair path),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanWindowCycle.DirectCycleFree LengthOK pair ↔
    ¬LengthOK (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathAttachment.pairCycleLength pair.left.first pair.left.second) ∧
      ¬LengthOK (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathAttachment.pairCycleLength pair.right.first pair.right.second) ∧
        (∀ (leftSide rightSide : Bool),
            ¬LengthOK
                (4 +
                  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathAttachment.positionDistance (pair.left.endpoint leftSide)
                    (pair.right.endpoint rightSide))) ∧
          (pair.left.first < pair.right.first →
              pair.right.first < pair.left.second →
                pair.left.second < pair.right.second →
                  ¬LengthOK (4 + (↑pair.right.first - ↑pair.left.first) + (↑pair.right.second - ↑pair.left.second))) ∧
            (pair.right.first < pair.left.first →
              pair.left.first < pair.right.second →
                pair.right.second < pair.left.second →
                  ¬LengthOK (4 + (↑pair.left.first - ↑pair.right.first) + (↑pair.left.second - ↑pair.right.second)))

stages.title: CT14 certificate-closed fan charge
stages.summary: CT14 scans the actual fan ports, computes the cubic-closed count, and verifies the exact quarter-charge identity 11-k-4c. The defining condition 4c+k≤11 yields nonnegative closed-neighbourhood charge.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx,
        Internal.PackedProblem.rank ctx.G ≤
            Internal.PackedProblem.rank (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.pack object) ∧
          Internal.VerifiedCertificateClosedFanChargePrefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.exists_verifiedCertificateClosedFanChargePrefix

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {Member : Type uMember} (profile : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CertificateClosedFanCharge.Profile Member)
  {problem : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} (context : StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext problem),
  profile.closedCount context ≤ FinEnum.card Member
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CertificateClosedFanCharge.Profile.closedCount_le_card

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {Member : Type uMember} (profile : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CertificateClosedFanCharge.Profile Member)
  {problem : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} (context : StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext problem),
  profile.closedCount context + profile.openCount context = FinEnum.card Member
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CertificateClosedFanCharge.Profile.count_partition

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{Member : Type uMember} →

        StructuralExhaustion.CT14.ExecutionResult (profile.capability problem) context

  (profile.run context).terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT14.Graph.Terminal.capacity

evidenceDeclarations.type:

  {problem : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} (context : StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext problem),
  profile.neighborhoodQuarterChargeLower context = 11 - ↑(FinEnum.card Member) - 4 * ↑(profile.closedCount context)

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {Member : Type uMember} (profile : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CertificateClosedFanCharge.Profile Member)

  4 * profile.closedCount context + FinEnum.card Member ≤ 11 → 0 ≤ profile.neighborhoodQuarterChargeLower context
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CertificateClosedFanCharge.Profile.neighborhoodQuarterChargeLower_nonnegative

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (center : V) (centerHigh : 4 ≤ object.degree center)
  (deletionCritical : ∀ (dart : object.graph.Dart), object.degree dart.toProd.1 = 3 ∨ object.degree dart.toProd.2 = 3)
  (Assigned : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.LocalCarrier V → Prop)
  (port : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HighCenterPort.Port object center),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.AssignedFanCharge.CubicClosed object center Assigned port ↔
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.AssignedFanCharge.firstAssigned object center centerHigh deletionCritical Assigned port ∧
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.AssignedFanCharge.secondAssigned object center centerHigh deletionCritical Assigned
        port
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.AssignedFanCharge.cubicClosed_iff_both_assigned

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (center : V) (centerHigh : 4 ≤ object.degree center)
  (deletionCritical : ∀ (dart : object.graph.Dart), object.degree dart.toProd.1 = 3 ∨ object.degree dart.toProd.2 = 3)
  (Assigned : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.LocalCarrier V → Prop)
  (assignedDecidable :
    (carrier : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.LocalCarrier V) → Decidable (Assigned carrier))
  (port : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HighCenterPort.Port object center),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.AssignedFanCharge.CubicClosed object center Assigned port →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.AssignedFanCharge.quarterCharge object center centerHigh deletionCritical Assigned
        assignedDecidable port =
      -1
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.AssignedFanCharge.quarterCharge_eq_neg_one_of_cubicClosed

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (center : V) (centerHigh : 4 ≤ object.degree center)
  (deletionCritical : ∀ (dart : object.graph.Dart), object.degree dart.toProd.1 = 3 ∨ object.degree dart.toProd.2 = 3)
  (Assigned : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.LocalCarrier V → Prop)
  (assignedDecidable :
    (carrier : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.LocalCarrier V) → Decidable (Assigned carrier))
  (port : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HighCenterPort.Port object center),
  ¬StructuralExhaustion.Graph.AssignedFanCharge.CubicClosed object center Assigned port →
    3 ≤
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.AssignedFanCharge.quarterCharge object center centerHigh deletionCritical Assigned
        assignedDecidable port

stages.title: CT14 positive-deficit marked-fan entry
stages.summary: Two actual compatible fan-closed ports force at least two cubic-closed neighbours. The previously proved marked-fan cap supplies k≤8, and the routed CT14 incidence ledger pays the resulting positive quarter-deficit with three units of slack.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx,
        Internal.PackedProblem.rank ctx.G ≤
            Internal.PackedProblem.rank (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.pack object) ∧
          Internal.VerifiedPositiveDeficitFanEntryPrefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.exists_verifiedPositiveDeficitFanEntryPrefix

evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.PositiveDeficitMarkedFan.massStage

evidenceDeclarations.type:

  (entry : Internal.PositiveDeficitMarkedFan ctx), ctx.G.object.degree entry.fan.center ≤ 8

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget}

  2 ≤
    FinEnum.card
      { vertex //
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPortMass.CubicClosedNeighbor
          (Internal.p13FanWindowProfile ctx entry.Assigned entry.assignedDecidable) vertex }
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.PositiveDeficitMarkedFan.two_le_closedCount

evidenceDeclarations.type:

    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPortMass.deficitNumerator (ctx.G.object.degree entry.fan.center)
      (FinEnum.card
        { vertex //
          StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPortMass.CubicClosedNeighbor
            (Internal.p13FanWindowProfile ctx entry.Assigned entry.assignedDecidable) vertex })
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.PositiveDeficitMarkedFan.positiveDeficit

evidenceDeclarations.type:

    entry.second ⋯ ⋯

evidenceDeclarations.type:

  (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanIncidence.run ⋯ ⋯
        (Internal.p13FanWindowProfile ctx entry.Assigned entry.assignedDecidable) entry.first entry.second ⋯).terminal =
    StructuralExhaustion.CT14.Graph.Terminal.capacity

evidenceDeclarations.type:

  (entry : Internal.PositiveDeficitMarkedFan ctx),
  3 ≤
    ↑(StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanIncidence.totalQuarterCredit
          (Internal.p13FanWindowProfile ctx entry.Assigned entry.assignedDecidable)) -
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPortMass.deficitNumerator (ctx.G.object.degree entry.fan.center)
        (FinEnum.card
          (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanIncidence.ClosedMember
            (Internal.p13FanWindowProfile ctx entry.Assigned entry.assignedDecidable)))

stages.title: Concrete positive-deficit Type B candidate fibre
stages.summary: The framework candidate fibre is generated from the literal 2c fan incidences: every window incidence is mandatory, reserve-used incidences are forbidden, and selected non-window incidences must pay the exact remaining CT14 demand.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx,
        Internal.PackedProblem.rank ctx.G ≤
            Internal.PackedProblem.rank (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.pack object) ∧
          Internal.VerifiedPositiveDeficitCandidatePrefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.exists_verifiedPositiveDeficitCandidatePrefix

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {Item : Type uItem} {Carrier : Type uCarrier}
  (profile : StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteWeightedSelection.Profile Item Carrier), Finite profile.Candidate
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteWeightedSelection.Profile.finite_candidate_fibre

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {base : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput V fun x => Unit}
  {object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V} {baseline : base.problem.Baseline object} {center : V}
  (fanProfile : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.FanWindowProfile V)
  (reserve : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.IncidenceReserve V)
  (candidate : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.Candidate fanProfile reserve)
  {incidence : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.Incidence fanProfile},
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanIncidence.incidenceKind fanProfile incidence =
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.IncidenceKind.window →
    incidence ∈ ↑candidate
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.Candidate.contains_every_window

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {base : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput V fun x => Unit}
  {object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V} {baseline : base.problem.Baseline object} {center : V}
  (fanProfile : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.FanWindowProfile V)
  (reserve : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.IncidenceReserve V)
  (candidate : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.Candidate fanProfile reserve)
  {incidence : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.Incidence fanProfile},
  incidence ∈ ↑candidate → ¬reserve.Used (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanIncidence.carrier fanProfile incidence)
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.Candidate.selected_reserve_free

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {base : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput V fun x => Unit}
  {object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V} {baseline : base.problem.Baseline object} {center : V}
  (fanProfile : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.FanWindowProfile V)
  (reserve : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.IncidenceReserve V)
  (candidate : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.Candidate fanProfile reserve),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanIncidence.remainingNonWindowDemand fanProfile ≤
    ∑ incidence ∈ ↑candidate,
      if
          StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanIncidence.incidenceKind fanProfile incidence =
            StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.IncidenceKind.nonWindow then
        2
      else 0
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.Candidate.nonWindow_payment

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u} →
  {base : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput V fun x => Unit} →
    {object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V} →
      {baseline : base.problem.Baseline object} →
        {center : V} →
          (fanProfile : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.FanWindowProfile V) →
            (reserve : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.IncidenceReserve V) →
              object.degree center ≤ 8 →
                (∀ (incidence : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.Incidence fanProfile),
                    ¬reserve.Used (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanIncidence.carrier fanProfile incidence)) →
                  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.Candidate fanProfile reserve

stages.title: Concrete certificate-closed Type B candidate fibre
stages.summary: The candidate items are the actual fan-neighbour ports. Their assigned-incidence quarter weight is a proved lower bound for induced-core charge; an exact external-shoulder correction accounts for decorative and window incidences. Reserve-used vertices are forbidden, and validity is nonnegative center-plus-selected-neighbour lower-bound charge.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx,
        Internal.PackedProblem.rank ctx.G ≤
            Internal.PackedProblem.rank (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.pack object) ∧
          Internal.VerifiedTypeBCandidateFibresPrefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.exists_verifiedTypeBCandidateFibresPrefix

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {Member : Type uMember} {Vertex : Type uVertex}
  (chargeProfile : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CertificateClosedFanCharge.Profile Member)
  (vertexDecidableEq : DecidableEq Vertex) (center : Vertex) (vertex : Member → Vertex)
  (reserve : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CertificateClosedFanCandidate.VertexReserve Vertex)
  {problem : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} (context : StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext problem),
  -↑(chargeProfile.closedCount context) + 3 * ↑(chargeProfile.openCount context) ≤
    ∑
      member ∈
        (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CertificateClosedFanCandidate.Profile.selectionProfile chargeProfile
            vertexDecidableEq center vertex reserve).allItems,
      chargeProfile.quarterCharge member

stages.title: Exact assigned charge, receiver discharge, and boundary split
stages.summary: Lean transfers every candidate weight and half-credit to literal induced-core charge and proves the exact post-selection decomposition. Independently of local resolution or B2 disjointness, deleting all actual high centers leaves a literal P13-free, core-free, subcubic graph. Its exact receiver overload gives the unconditional bound -TypeBNet ≤ 21 assigned-surplus + TypeA-overload.

          Internal.VerifiedTypeBPostLedgerPrefix ctx

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {Demand : Type uDemand} {Carrier : Type uCarrier}
  (profile : StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteRefinedLedger.Profile Demand Carrier) {selected : List Demand}
  (choice : profile.Choice selected) (refinedSupport : (demand : Demand) → profile.Candidate demand → Finset Carrier),
  (∀ (demand : Demand) (candidate : profile.Candidate demand),
      refinedSupport demand candidate ⊆ profile.carrierSupport demand candidate) →
    ∀ (left right : { demand // demand ∈ selected }),
      ↑left ≠ ↑right →
        Disjoint (refinedSupport (↑left) (choice.entry left)) (refinedSupport (↑right) (choice.entry right))

          ∑ vertex ∈ used, profile.coreQuarterChargeAt vertex +

            ∑ vertex ∈ profile.remainingCore used, profile.coreQuarterChargeAt vertex

stages.title: CT12 two-degenerate sparse envelope
stages.summary: Deletion criticality supplies a cubic vertex. The no-proper-core theorem makes its literal complement 2-degenerate; CT12 audits the proof-selected elimination list exactly once per remaining vertex, yielding m ≤ 2n-2 and the exact sparse slack/surplus identities.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →

stages.summary: The sparse-envelope output first activates every exact CT6 surplus slot through its CT2-derived return and open-suppression or triangular response. The exact cubic-baseline skeleton count and integer bit budget are then computed from n; CT15 certifies the canonical baseline-demand ledger. No linear deficit estimate is assumed.

stages.title: CT15 exact free/blocked pair responses
stages.summary: The exact activated-slot schedule is paired once, each pair is classified by a finite local blocker scan, and every free pair receives one proof-carrying shortest connector. CT15 then verifies full rank under the audited admissible-quotient contract; boundary and target mismatches are raw-proposal exits and cannot occur after admission.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx,
        Internal.PackedProblem.rank ctx.G ≤
            Internal.PackedProblem.rank (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.pack object) ∧
          Internal.VerifiedSparsePairResponsePrefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.exists_verifiedSparsePairResponsePrefix

evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteBlockerLedger.FamilyProfile.blocked_card_add_free_card

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {Pair : Type uPair} {Candidate : Type uCandidate}
  (family : StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteBlockerLedger.FamilyProfile Pair Candidate)
  (blocked : { pair // family.HasBlocker pair }),
  (family.firstBlocker blocked).value ∈ (family.scan.candidates ↑blocked).values ∧
    family.scan.Blocks (↑blocked) (family.firstBlocker blocked).value ∧
      ∀ candidate ∈ (family.firstBlocker blocked).before, ¬family.scan.Blocks (↑blocked) candidate
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteBlockerLedger.FamilyProfile.firstBlocker_sound

stages.title: CT9 pair dispatch and free-anchor route
stages.summary: CT9 executes the exact node-130 pre-retokenization dispatch by first selected port and a five-way role. Blocked pairs retain their canonical first-hit record for node 134; blocker-free pairs receive the literal freeAnchor role and enter the primitive selected-port audit with their retained shortest connector. The downstream capacity-token map is not part of this stage.

          Internal.VerifiedAllPairTokenRoutingPrefix ctx

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} {Item : Type uItem} {Token : Type uToken} {Role : Type uRole}
  (tokens : FinEnum Token) (roles : FinEnum Role) (token : Item → Token) (role : Item → Role)
  (context : StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext P) (items : StructuralExhaustion.Core.OrderedCollection Item),
  items.values.length =
    (List.map

          StructuralExhaustion.CT9.fibreCount
            (StructuralExhaustion.CT9.TokenRoleLedger.capability P Item tokens roles token role)
            (StructuralExhaustion.CT9.TokenRoleLedger.input context items) labelValue)

stages.title: CT9 capacity-token and exact join ledger
stages.summary: The exact blocked output is retokenized by the manuscript priority map; the selected induced-P13 packing supplies the window-join identity; the existing free-anchor side and the blocked side form one exact 25-role partition of every scheduled pair.

          Internal.VerifiedCapacityTokenPrefix ctx

stages.title: CT9 exact coupled class overload
stages.summary: For every fixed threshold triple, CT9 compares the literal complete surplus-pair count with the exact 25-role class capacity. Positive excess returns an actual overloaded fibre and its token-class route; the other side proves the explicit quadratic surplus bound.

      refinedSupport demand candidate ⊆ profile.carrierSupport demand candidate) →

        Disjoint (refinedSupport (↑left) (choice.entry left)) (refinedSupport (↑right) (choice.entry right))

example.title: Erdős Problem 64
example.summary: A partial proof with exact Mersenne returns, lexicographic minimal selection, CT2 criticality and bridge contraction, CT3 boundaried uncompressibility, an HSS-forced induced-P13 CT1 stage, a routed maximum-packing CT12 remainder stage, the complete CT10 P13 attachment-label algebra, a polynomial ordered CT6 degree-surplus ledger with exact per-slot activation, the CT15 baseline and free/blocked pair-response stages, the exact CT9 capacity-token partition, and the exact 25-role coupled overload/class decision, together with the framework-native local-port and Type B ledgers retained by the same selected graph.

proofSteps.workBound: Statement boundary only; no computation is performed.
proofSteps.title: Official theorem boundary
proofSteps.scopeNotes: The public statement is exact, but the current verified workflow is explicitly a partial prefix and does not yet prove this proposition.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: The public Lean proposition uses the same finite simple graph and asks for exactly the power-of-two cycle asserted by the manuscript's main theorem.

declarationGroups.title: Public mathematical statement
declarationGroups.explanation: This declaration is the exact Mathlib-facing proposition that the completed formalization must prove.

declarations.type: Prop
declarations.name: Erdos64EG.OfficialStatement

proofSteps.workBound: The exponent search is bounded by the candidate cycle length.
proofSteps.title: Faithful executable target
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Finite enumeration and adjacency decisions control execution order only; they do not introduce a second graph representation or weaken the theorem.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Lean equips the same Mathlib graph with finite inspection data and replaces the unbounded exponent quantifier by a bounded decidable search, then proves that this executable target is equivalent to the official conclusion.

declarationGroups.title: Encoding and equivalence bridge
declarationGroups.explanation: The static input supplies deterministic finite data; the two equivalence theorems prove that neither the cycle predicate nor its exponent range has changed.

proofSteps.title: Mersenne return target algebra
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This step proves the manuscript's target dictionary and executes both CT1 branches; it does not yet rule out the target-avoiding branch.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Removing one edge from a power-of-two cycle leaves a return path whose length is one less than a power of two. Conversely, adjoining the root edge to such a return produces the target cycle. CT1 records either a supplied return or universal avoidance.
proofSteps.formalStatement: G \text{ has a } C_{2^j} \iff \exists e,\ 2^j-1 \in R_e(G)

proofSteps.title: No proper minimum-degree-three core
proofSteps.scopeNotes: The framework checks one proof-specified proper subgraph at a time; the theorem universally quantifies over certificates and does not enumerate subgraphs.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: If a proper subgraph still had minimum degree at least three, target avoidance would be inherited and it would be a lexicographically smaller counterexample. Certified CT2 reduction formalizes this contradiction for one arbitrary supplied proper subgraph.

declarationGroups.title: Mathematical conclusion
declarationGroups.explanation: This theorem is the direct Lean form of the manuscript lemma: every proper finite subgraph of the selected counterexample has minimum degree at most two.

proofSteps.title: Edge-deletion criticality
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This matches the manuscript deletion argument. The registered route is implementation provenance, not an additional mathematical hypothesis.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: An edge with both endpoints of degree at least four could be deleted while preserving minimum degree three, and deletion cannot create a new cycle. Minimality therefore forces every edge to touch a degree-three vertex.
proofSteps.formalStatement: xy \in E(G) \Longrightarrow d_G(x)=3 \lor d_G(y)=3;\qquad V_{\ge 4}(G) \text{ is independent}

declarationGroups.title: Degree-three endpoint consequences
declarationGroups.explanation: The first theorem proves the degree-three endpoint alternative for every edge; the second derives independence of the vertices of degree at least four.

proofSteps.workBound: Proof-level literal gluing and one certified reduction; no universe of contexts, graphs, or replacements is enumerated.
proofSteps.title: Boundaried replacement and uncompressibility
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This covers nodes [11]--[14] for normalized decompositions with nonempty boundary and the complete certificate-local routing at nodes [36]--[39]. A context mismatch yields the exact target-defective residual; a context-universal at-atom realization executes CT3 and contradicts minimality; an enlarged-support residual is retained unchanged for node [40]. Empty-boundary closed representatives remain a separate later manuscript branch.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: A proper boundaried atom cannot be replaced by a strictly smaller representative with the same boundary degrees and no worse response in every compatible outside context. Such a replacement would preserve the baseline and target avoidance and contradict minimality.

declarationGroups.title: Boundary and replacement theorems

proofSteps.workBound: Zero checks on the avoiding proof and one check on a supplied induced embedding; no path or tuple universe is enumerated.
proofSteps.title: Forced induced P₁₃
proofSteps.scopeNotes: The corollary is fully formalized relative to the explicitly isolated HSS theorem. The app must display that trust boundary separately from kernel-proved consequences.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: CT1 tests for an induced P₁₃. Its avoiding residual is literal P₁₃-freeness, which the Hegde--Sandeep--Shashank theorem turns into a forbidden power-of-two cycle. Therefore the minimal counterexample contains an induced P₁₃, recorded by a proof-carrying graph embedding.

declarationGroups.title: Trusted external closure
declarationGroups.explanation: This isolated axiom is exactly the cited Hegde--Sandeep--Shashank theorem: finite P₁₃-free minimum-degree-three graphs contain a power-of-two cycle. It is the sole trusted external theorem in the repository.

declarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (G : SimpleGraph V) [inst : Fintype V] [inst_1 : DecidableRel G.Adj],
  3 ≤ G.minDegree →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathFree G 13 →
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HasCycleWithLength G fun length => ∃ exponent, 2 ≤ exponent ∧ length = 2 ^ exponent
declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.External.HegdeSandeepShashank.p13Free_hasPowerOfTwoCycle

proofSteps.workBound: Exactly p₁₃ CT12 iterations, at most |V(G)|; the universe of embeddings and packings is not materialized.
proofSteps.title: Maximum induced-P₁₃ packing and remainder
proofSteps.scopeNotes: The packing and P₁₃-free remainder clauses are implemented. Nodes [25]--[26] consume the exact predecessor ceiling p₁₃ ≤ U₁₃ and derive the certified finite floor |V(G)| - 13U₁₃ ≤ |R| on the identical CT12 remainder. Producing the numerical ceiling remains node [24]'s separate task.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: A maximum vertex-disjoint family of induced P₁₃ copies is selected. Maximality makes the complementary induced graph P₁₃-free, and the supports give the exact partition |R| + 13p₁₃ = |V(G)|.

declarationGroups.title: Maximum packing and remainder theorems

proofSteps.workBound: Fifteen fixed 399×399 bit relations and 91 quadratic barrier scans, split into 15 independently cached audit shards. The public CT10 classification uses 196 candidate checks; no graph family or Boolean cube is enumerated.
proofSteps.title: Complete 91-barrier P13 curvature certificate
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [21] is complete on the exact bounded node-[19] constructor. Node [160] isolates the still-open positive realization/gluing package in its verified realized/open dichotomy and is not cited by this step. The next nodes [22]--[24] perform only their exact finite density split and proof-carrying handoffs. The strict constructor remains the already verified Part-X sparse-pressure route.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: On the bounded constructor of the exact node-[19] surplus split, CT10 classifies all 196 ordered candidate length pairs and retains exactly the 91 pairs a,b≥1 with a+b≤14. Fifteen fixed 399-row relation matrices are audited bit-for-bit against the graph attachment semantics. Independent shards audit every accepted safe and composition-flat count against the reusable finite-relation barrier, including the existing 543958/432672/111286 scale-(1,1) counts. Exact integer arithmetic proves that the product barrier is larger than 2^118, without evaluating a logarithm or assuming any Boolean-state realization.

declarationGroups.title: Sparse bit relation and graph semantics

proofSteps.workBound: Proof-level excluded-middle split only; no states, assignments, paths, graphs, contexts, or Boolean cubes are enumerated.
proofSteps.title: Typed node-[160] realization/open-requirement dichotomy
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Green means the node's exact dichotomy and both output contracts are implemented. It does not claim the positive package is inhabited: the open constructor remains at node [160], and only the realized constructor may enter node [22].
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [160] follows the manuscript handoff without deriving Boolean realization from the 91-row count. The independently defined local states are finite graphs on the fixed vertex type with the selected window, local support preservation, minimum degree, target avoidance, and responses computed from the node-[21] curvature relation. A proof-level split either carries simultaneous realization and support-commuting global gluing for those states, or retains the exact nonexistence of that package with the named downstream producers.

declarationGroups.title: Exact realized package or certified open requirement
declarationGroups.explanation: Local and global completion meanings are fixed before the certificate: they carry actual finite graphs, graph-local support preservation, baseline and target admissibility, and node-[21] barrier responses. The certificate supplies only finite interpreted families, realization, restriction/recovery, and support-level commuting gluing. The complementary constructor stores the exact package negation and predecessor-owned requirement.

Node [23] retains the exact overflow input for the later entropy contradiction; it does not claim that contradiction here. Node [24] retains the exact complementary finite cap; the later strict-quarter and high-entropy refinements remain separate consumers. No completion state, Boolean assignment, graph family, or ambient universe is enumerated.

The strict reverse is the node-[23] overflow payload passed to the later entropy consumer; the complementary inequality is the node-[24] finite density-ceiling payload passed to the remainder and hot/cold consumers.

proofSteps.workBound: Worst-case reference budget for one supplied selected window: at most 8192·n assignment/state vector comparisons, each comparing thirteen adjacency bits; equivalently at most 106496·n bit comparisons. The subsequent corridor/path work at node [159] is excluded.
proofSteps.title: Pointwise actual P13 attachment cold fork
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [158] is a pointwise actual thirteen-bit adjacency scan. The production same-context adapter maps every exact node-[21] packed window through this cold fork and node [159], and proves that its four structural subledgers partition exactly p13 windows. This neither proves nor refutes the still-white node-[160] 91-coordinate realization edge [21]→[22], and it yields no entropy or density bound.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: For one exact selected induced-P13 window, the classifier scans actual outside vertices against the thirteen literal path-adjacency bits. A hot certificate would realize the all-true assignment; positions 0 and 2 then produce a four-cycle, contradicting target avoidance. The classifier therefore returns its canonical missing assignment and retains the exact same selected window.

proofSteps.title: Same-window P13 structural frontier
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [159] is green only for this pointwise four-way classifier. The window-surplus and corridor-high outputs remain typed open handoffs, and the quiet output is only ColdStructuralGerm, not a constant ColdBoundedGerm. No density, cold-mass, bounded-overlap, or aggregate nodes-[153]–[157] claim follows. Node [160]'s separate realized/open dichotomy supplies no positive package here.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: The exact node-[158] cold fork indexes a graph-owned continuation on the same selected window. It returns exactly one of four constructors: a high-degree window position, a dyadic root-cycle hit, the first high-degree corridor vertex with its clean prefix, or a quiet structural germ carrying only an ambient-size support bound.

declarations.type:
∀ {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget}
  {previous : Internal.VerifiedP13MultiScaleCurvaturePrefix ctx} {window : Internal.P13ActualSelectedWindow ctx}
  (fork : Internal.P13ActualAttachmentColdFork ctx previous window),
  (∃ position,
      ∃ (high : 3 < ctx.G.object.degree (↑window position)),
        Internal.runP13SameWindowStructuralFrontier fork =
          Internal.P13SameWindowStructuralFrontier.surplus position high) ∨
    (∃ stub,
        ∃ (same : stub.window = Internal.selectedConnectorWindowIndex window),
          ∃ hit,
            ∃ (targetProof :
              StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.F1 ⋯ Internal.PowerOfTwoLength stub hit.value),
              ∃ target,
                ∃ (targetExact :
                  target =
                    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.targetHitOfF1 ⋯ Internal.PowerOfTwoLength stub
                      hit.value targetProof),
                  Internal.runP13SameWindowStructuralFrontier fork =
                    Internal.P13SameWindowStructuralFrontier.dyadicTargetHit stub same hit targetProof target
                      targetExact) ∨
      (∃ stub,
          ∃ (same : stub.window = Internal.selectedConnectorWindowIndex window),
            ∃ hit,
              ∃ (noTarget :
                ¬StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.F1 ⋯ Internal.PowerOfTwoLength stub hit.value) (high
                : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.F4 stub hit.value),
                ∃ handoff,
                  ∃ (handoffExact :
                    handoff =
                      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.surplusHandoffOfF4 ⋯ stub hit.value high),
                    Internal.runP13SameWindowStructuralFrontier fork =
                      Internal.P13SameWindowStructuralFrontier.corridorHighDegree stub same hit noTarget high handoff
                        handoffExact) ∨
        ∃ stub,
          ∃ (same : stub.window = Internal.selectedConnectorWindowIndex window) (noEvent :
            ∀ stage ∈ ((Erdos64EG.Internal.CorridorProfile✝ ctx).stages stub).values,
              ¬(Erdos64EG.Internal.CorridorProfile✝ ctx).Event stub stage),
            ∃ germ,
              Internal.runP13SameWindowStructuralFrontier fork =
                Internal.P13SameWindowStructuralFrontier.quiet stub same noEvent germ

proofSteps.title: Normalized one-return boundary rejoin
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [167] is only a proof-level one-return normalization. Its exact output feeds verified packed-support transition [168], whose all-inside branch then feeds verified owner-change node [169]. Node [167] itself supplies no owner table or cross-window token, successor/second stub/component path [170], iteration or termination argument, D4–D7 coordinate, CT3 input, or density theorem.

proofSteps.title: Normalized-return packed-support transition
proofSteps.scopeNotes: The union is over all ambient-cubic selected windows, not the manuscript's selected cold subfamily. Its all-inside branch feeds verified owner-change node [169], while its exact first-transition branch feeds verified component boundary schedule [170]. No aggregate cold mass, iteration, D4–D7 statement, CT3 execution, or density estimate follows.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Equality with node [167]'s computed normalized result fixes one ambient simple return ending in a selected ambient-cubic window support. A graph-owned ordered edge scan either proves every return-support vertex lies in the union of all ambient-cubic selected-window supports, or returns the first membership transition with its oriented crossing, exact BoundaryStub, outside endpoint, and induced-remainder component.
proofSteps.formalStatement: \operatorname{supp}(\Gamma')\subseteq U_{\rm ac}\quad\lor\quad\exists\text{ first }uv:\quad u\in U_{\rm ac},\quad v\notin U_{\rm ac}

declarations.type:

  {previous : Internal.VerifiedP13MultiScaleCurvaturePrefix ctx} {window : Internal.P13ActualSelectedWindow ctx}
  {fork : Internal.P13ActualAttachmentColdFork ctx previous window} {quiet : Internal.P13SameWindowQuietOutput fork}
  {short : Internal.P13SameWindowComputedShort fork quiet} {input : Internal.P13SameWindowNormalizedBoundaryInput short}
  (computed : Internal.P13SameWindowComputedNormalizedReturnBoundary input),
  (∃ (supportSubset :
      ∀ vertex ∈ computed.graphInput.path.support,
        vertex ∈ StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdSkeleton.deletedWindowVertices ctx.G.object),
      Internal.runP13SameWindowNormalizedReturnPackedSupportTransition computed =
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdSkeletonBoundaryTransition.Result.allInside supportSubset) ∨
    ∃ hit crossing stub,
      ∃ (stubExact : stub = crossing.boundaryStub),
        ∃ endpoint,
          ∃ (endpointExact : endpoint = stub.endpoint),
            ∃ component,
              ∃ (componentExact : component = StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdSkeleton.component stub),
                Internal.runP13SameWindowNormalizedReturnPackedSupportTransition computed =
                  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdSkeletonBoundaryTransition.Result.firstTransition hit
                    crossing stub stubExact endpoint endpointExact component componentExact

declarationGroups.title: Exact local work and polynomial bound

    13 * StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.packingNumber object * FinEnum.card V +
        13 * StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.packingNumber object +
      26 * StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.packingNumber object * input.path.length

proofSteps.title: Component boundary schedule and BFS path
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [170] belongs only to node [161]'s short first-transition branch and feeds verified one-state component observation [173]. That branch is incompatible with the verified long node-[163]--[164] branch and its white full-semantic consumer [179]; there is no edge from this short branch to [164] or [179]. Node [170] supplies no D4–D7 label, repetition, Boolean or cold-family semantics, iteration, target closure, CT3 execution, or density estimate.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Equality with node [168]'s computed first-transition result fixes the exact boundary stub and returned outside component. The same computed outside BFS finset drives a proof-carrying exit scan, the explicit finite window-slot search, the complete incident-stub schedule, its true cyclic successor, and the declared-order shortest BFS path between the two retained boundary neighbours.

declarationGroups.title: Complete visible ledger and cubic bound
declarationGroups.explanation: The exact ledger includes the return scan, explicit slot first-hit search, both BFS computations, incident-token filtering, and component-object restriction; each BFS budget is separately polynomial.

declarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V}
  (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathComponentBoundarySchedule.Input object),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathComponentBoundarySchedule.visibleChecks input =
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.checks object +
                13 * StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.packingNumber object +
              input.ambientReturn.length *
                FinEnum.card (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdSkeleton.OutsideVertex object) +
            (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathComponentBoundarySchedule.outsideBfsProfile input).budget
              (FinEnum.card (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdSkeleton.OutsideVertex object)) +
          FinEnum.card (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdSkeleton.OutsideVertex object) ^ 2 +
        FinEnum.card (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.Token object) *
          (2 + 13 * StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.packingNumber object +
            FinEnum.card (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdSkeleton.OutsideVertex object)) +
      (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathComponentBoundarySchedule.bfsProfile input).budget
        (FinEnum.card ↑(StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdSkeleton.component input.anchor).supp)

proofSteps.workBound: Exactly 2n+13 visible checks and at most 15(n+1). The independently computed BFS path is inherited from node [170]; no path family is generated, ordered, or scanned.
proofSteps.title: One component D1--D3 observation
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [173] constructs one state only. Its MissingD4D7 residual feeds the verified cyclic component D1–D3 ledger split [174], which in turn feeds the white D4–D7 reconstruction/coarse-repeat consumer [175]. Neither edge enters long-branch node [164] or [179]. Node [173] itself proves no state sequence, repetition, D4–D7 reconstruction, CT3 compression, Boolean or cold-family semantics, target closure, or density estimate.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [173] consumes node [170]'s exact two-boundary component schedule and independently computes its declared-order BFS-tree shortest path. Equality to that one computed path gives the observation interface's rank; no path family is ordered or scanned. The two literal degrees, two Fin 13 offsets, and connector length form one genuine State (Fin 0), while the empty local response honestly retains MissingD4D7 reconstruction.

declarationGroups.title: Computed-path observation and genuine empty-coordinate state
declarationGroups.explanation: The graph package independently computes the declared-order BFS-tree shortest path and ranks a supplied candidate only by equality with that computed path. It then materializes the two boundary observations, projects the unique Fin 0 local response, and exposes MissingD4D7 rather than inventing later coordinates.

proofSteps.title: Cyclic component D1--D3 ledger split
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [174] proves only the local exhaustive split. Its repeated branch records equality of two observed coarse D1--D3 states but does not prove full response equivalence or CT3 removability; its bounded branch proves only schedule length at most Qbase. The D4--D7 reconstruction or coarse-repeat consumer remains white node [175]. No edge enters long-branch node [164] or [179], and no ambient State universe is enumerated.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [174] requires the exact typed node-[173] residual and retains its state as the anchor row. It traverses node [170]'s complete incident-stub schedule with the stored true cyclic successor and computes one local D1--D3 row per scheduled stub. A finite collision search over only those observed rows returns either two distinct rows with the same coarse state or a proof that the schedule has length at most Qbase.

proofSteps.title: D4--D7 availability and coarse-repeat routing
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [175] classifies only the availability of graph-derived D4--D7 clauses and retains exact refinement residuals. It proves no full D4--D7 compatible-response equivalence, CT8 removal, certified smaller object, second connector, cycle, or target closure; those implications remain white node [180].
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [175] consumes the exact node-[174] result. A repeated coarse row pair is passed unchanged to a promoted CT10 refinement; a bounded schedule scans only its stored rows and returns a complete D4--D7 family or its first typed missing row, which is also routed through CT10 on the actual branch context.
proofSteps.formalStatement: \text{node-[174] result}\Longrightarrow\text{routed coarse repeat, reconstructed rows, or routed first missing row}

declarationGroups.title: Exact node-174 consumer, two CT10 routes, and local work

declarationGroups.title: Local cubic work bound
declarationGroups.explanation: One marker costs at most four times the cube of the ambient vertex count: two adjacency decisions classify each raw triple and at most two boundary-role responses evaluate each retained wedge. The exact coarse and bounded constructors therefore cost at most 8n^3 and 4n^3 respectively.

  (source194 :
    transition.D4EvaluatorConstructionSource source174 source175 source180 source182 source185 source188 source191),
  match transition.runD4Evaluation source174 source175 source180 source182 source185 source188 source191 source194 with
  |
  Internal.P13SameWindowFirstTransitionBoundaryInput.D4EvaluationOutput.coarse b f s fc sc fr sr fx sx fb sb
      firstEvaluation secondEvaluation =>
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathComponentD4.visibleChecks b.routed.residual.repetition.firstInput +

proofSteps.workBound: At most n²+Qbase(n+1) visible checks using one prepared slot inventory, one stored owner lookup per path vertex, and one stored-owner comparison per edge.
proofSteps.title: Ambient-cubic owner sequence and first cross-window edge
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Owners range over all ambient-cubic selected windows, not the manuscript cold subfamily. Its exact first-cross-window package feeds verified zero-check handoff node [171]. Node [169] constructs no boundary successor, target-cycle closure, cold aggregate, D4–D7 coordinate, CT3 input, or density estimate.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Equality with node [168]'s all-inside result fixes the same normalized return. A once-prepared finite inventory assigns and stores the unique ambient-cubic selected-window owner of every return vertex. The single-window result contradicts the original external stub neighbour, so the surviving result is the first owner-change edge with two exact opposite cross-window tokens.
proofSteps.formalStatement: \exists i<|\Gamma'|:\quad W_i\ne W_{i+1}\quad\text{and two exact cross-window tokens}

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [171] is the exact terminal residual of the computed all-inside branch. It proves no second connector, repeated-owner structure, cycle, cold-family membership, demand or capacity bound, successor, target closure, CT execution, or density estimate.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [171] consumes node [169]'s complete first-cross-window package. A reusable typed route projects the two already computed endpoint tokens, proves they are distinct opposite oriented contributions of the same literal edge, and retains both exact cross-window subtypes without performing another search. This is the terminal residual of the computed all-inside branch.
